observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor reconstitute the Inventory Committee
ensuring that it is composed of adequate number of members to be able
to complete the physical inventory of the PPE in three months or less, and who shall
be temporarily relieved of all their regular duties to devote their full time in the
conduct of the physical inventory taking until the same is completed, in
compliance with COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020. After which, it
is recommended that the City Mayor direct the:
a. Inventory Committee to prepare a Physical Inventory Plan and accordingly
conduct a comprehensive physical inventory of all PPE of the City and prepare the
corresponding Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment
(RPCPPE) following the guidelines set forth in said COA Circular; and
b. General Services Officer to update the General Services Office’s property records
based on the RPCPPE and reconcile them with accounting records to come up
with the reconciled balances of PPE accounts to be considered as the correct
balance of the agency’s PPEs.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct all City Department Heads and their
respective Supply Officers to conduct physical count of all unissued,
undistributed or unconsumed supplies and materials in their custody as of year-
end and correspondingly prepare RPCI and submit the same to General Services
Office (GSO) for consolidation and to the City Accountant for adjustment of the
inventory account in the books of account, if warranted.

We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to draw a
Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) debiting Hostels and Dormitories and
Slaughterhouses and crediting Buildings and Markets accounts by P21,727,520.00
and P1,452,428.00, respectively, together with the corresponding Accumulated
Depreciations.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the City Accountant to cause the
immediate reversion to the unappropriated surplus of the general fund of the
accounts payables which have been outstanding for at least two years against
which no actual claim, administrative or judicial, has been filed and those which
are not supported with complete documentation.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City General Services Officer to
prepare the Property Inventory Form listing all the insurable properties of the City
and submit the same to the Office of the Auditor and GIF, GSIS not later than April
30 of each year, and insure subject assets with the GSIS in compliance with the
foregoing laws, rules and regulations.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant and other
concerned officials to submit to the Audit Team the lacking documents to
facilitate the timely auditorial review and technical evaluation of the contracts for
the purchase of the 11 lots.

We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that the City Mayor direct the
General Service Officer (GSO) and other concerned officials to make a study or
analysis of the costs and benefits of disposing or repairing the defective vehicles.
For vehicles determined or which could be established to be beyond repair, we
recommended that their disposal be immediately initiated by the City in
accordance with existing rules and regulations on disposal of unserviceable
properties.

For serviceable vehicles and those which could still be reasonably repaired, we
again recommended that the City Mayor require the GSO to exert more effort to
find the relevant Deeds of Sale, Certificate of Registration and other necessary
documents and to immediately comply with other LTO’s requirements to facilitate the
vehicles’ registration in the City’s name.

We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City Accountant to return to the
Bureau of Treasury the balances of the funds transfers from various NGAs that
had been dormant for more than five years or the purpose of which had already
been achieved by the City.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the City Planning and Development
Coordinator and City Engineer to prepare a catch-up plan for the immediate
completion of the project “Construction of Tenement Housing Project Building II”.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct:
a. the City Disaster Risk Reduction Management Council (CDRRMC) to identify
and incorporate, under a separate caption, in the correct format of
LDRRMFIP, the specific projects and activities to be charged against the
unexpended previous years’ LDRRMF;
b. the CDRRMC to ensure that supplemental budget or any revision in the
LDRRMF budget is supported by a revised AIP/LDRRMFIP approved by the
Sanggunian Panlungsod and City Mayor and which should, thereafter, be the
basis for the release and utilization of the LDRRMF for the identified new
expenditure items; and
c. all concerned officials to ensure that all projects and programs in the
LDRRMFIP are fully implemented.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct all members of the GAD Focal Point
System to undergo trainings on HGDG to build their competence in the conduct of
gender analysis of programs and projects for purposes of GAD budget attribution.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the City Engineer and other
concerned officials to ensure that the required project signboards and/or public
notices are displayed at the project location and that a report of the City’s
compliance with the posting requirement is submitted to the COA Audit Team
within 10 days after the award of the infrastructure project or before the start of
the activity in adherence to the provisions of COA Circular No. 2013-004.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the BAC to undertake the
procurement of the COVID-19 related goods using the emergency procurement
(Negotiated Procurement) modality required under GPPB Circular No. 01-2020 to
ensure prompt deliveries of goods to beneficiaries.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the City Social Worker Development
Officer (CSWDO) and City Treasurer to provide the Audit Team with proof that the
financial assistance intended for the 126 households from the six barangays of
the City amounting to P295,000.00 were actually received by the intended
beneficiaries, otherwise, the amount must be returned by the concerned barangays
and consequently be returned by the City to the source agency.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant to submit the 44
DVs and their supporting documents with an aggregate amount of P84,396,602.26
to the COA Audit Team to facilitate their audit, otherwise the corresponding
transactions will be suspended in audit.

Henceforth, it is recommended that the City Accountant be instructed to submit to
the Audit Team within the first ten days of the ensuing month all paid vouchers
and supporting documents pursuant to Section 7.2 of the 2009 RRSA of COA.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant and the OIC, General Services Office to analyze and reconcile the PPE discrepancy between the Accounting Office and the General Services Office (GSO) and to make the necessary adjustments.
observations.managementAction: There was already an on-going analysis and reconciliation of PPE discrepancies between the GSO and the Accounting office.
observations.reason: Analysis and reconciliation of PPE is still on-going.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant to make the necessary adjusting entries to recognize the assets in the General Fund and Trust Fund books, in accordance with GAM for LGUs, Volume I.
observations.managementAction: The corresponding adjusting entries were prepared and recorded in the General and Trust Fund books.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant to prepare a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to reclassify the balance of advance collection of business taxes from the Other Payables account to Other Deferred Credits account to reflect accurate and reliable account balances.
observations.managementAction: The balance previously recorded as Other Payables was already reclassified to Other Deferred Credits Account.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the: a) Acting City Treasurer to require the business taxpayers to submit Sworn Statement of all their gross sales/receipts not only from the City Government for the preceding Calendar Year and other records/information for determination of gross receipts; b) City Accountant to submit to the Acting City Treasurer a List of Payments made to contractors/suppliers who transacted with the City Government in the preceding calendar year to serve as one of the bases in the assessment of business taxes in the current year; c) OIC-Business Permit and Licensing Officer to compute any possible interest and surcharges; and d) Acting City Treasurer to re-assess the declared gross receipts of the concerned suppliers in calendar year (CY) 2020 and validate the reported under collection totaling P2,975,335.82 and to enforce the collection of underpayments including penalties and surcharges, if there are any, pursuant to the rates provided in the City’s Revised Revenue Code, otherwise, Notice of Charge will be issued by the Audit Team.
observations.managementAction: Letters were sent to the concerned taxpayers. List of payments were submitted. The Audit Team was furnished with copies of the sworn statements from concerned taxpayers. The Acting City Treasurer submitted the computation. With Sangguniang Panlungsod (SP) Resolution to enforce collection and waiver of penalties.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to explain/justify the discrepancies on the prices of the basic commodities that the City procured in response to the COVID-19 and their suggested retail prices during the price freeze implementation, to prevent suspension and/or disallowance in audit.
observations.managementAction: The General Services Officer (GSO) and other concerned officials issued a certification citing that the purchase of basic commodities in response to the COVID-19 were done according to the procurement rules and regulations, all supplies, especially rice, came all the way from Province of Isabela because of the big requirements of the City, during that time, there were no available supplies within the area and the city has no other option but to procure the basic commodities available, more particularly that market survey or canvass had been done, preferably citing the names of stores/suppliers in and around the locality, and that it was determined that there were no available stocks or no supplier was capable of delivering the needed goods.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant to properly record the purchases of the Welfare Goods under the Welfare Goods for Distribution account, and the distributed portion to Welfare Goods Expenses, which will be closed out to the Transfer from General Fund of Unspent DRRMF account at the end of the year for proper monitoring of receipts, issuances, and balances of goods procured out of the prior year's unexpended balances of LDRRMF.
observations.managementAction: The City Accountant has properly recorded the purchases of the welfare goods under the Welfare Goods for Distribution account, and the distributed portion under the Welfare Goods for Distribution account, and the distributed portion under Welfare Goods Expenses, which will be closed out to the Transfer From General Fund of Unspent DRRMF account, which will be closed out to the Transfer from General Fund of Unspent DRRMF account at the end of the year for proper monitoring of receipts, issuances, and balances of goods procured out of the prior year’s unexpended balances of LDRRMF.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Budget Officer to ensure that only expenditures directly related to disaster risk management are charged against the LDRRMF, while administrative expenses are charged instead against the General Fund frontliners who physically report to their respective City Offices.
observations.managementAction: The OIC-City Budget Officer issued a certification stating the status of the budget of the City, wherein the LDRRMF was the available budget that can be used to pay the frontliners who physically report to the respective City Offices.
observations.reason: The City Accountant has ensured that LDRRMF was the available budget that can be used to pay the frontliners who physically report to the respective City Offices; however, full compliance on charging only disaster-related expenditures was not fully achieved.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the BAC Secretariat to submit to the Audit Team the justifications on its inability to comply with the posting requirement under GPPB Circular No. 01-2020, for further evaluation.
observations.managementAction: The BAC Secretariat implemented the recommendation in CY 2021 and submitted the audit documents. The BAC Secretariat issued a certification justifying that they were unable to post the NOA in the City’s Website because during that time, the City’s internet connection (PLDT DSL) were down/unserviceable and they followed the procedure for online purchases under the Bayanihan Act by posting the NOA in conspicuous premises.
observations.reason: The BAC implemented the recommendation and submitted documents but were not able to post the NOA in the City’s Website because internet connection was down/unserviceable at that time.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our previous year’s audit recommendation that the City Mayor direct the Head, BAC Secretariat to comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-001 on the submission of copies of contracts and their supporting documents to the Auditor within five working days from the execution thereof, to facilitate the prompt review and evaluation of the contracts, and the early communication to Management on the deficiencies noted, if there are any.
observations.managementAction: The BAC implemented the recommendation in CY 2021. The required contracts and their supporting documents were submitted to COA office; however, those submitted were not within five working days from execution of the contract.
observations.reason: Copies of contracts and their supporting documents were submitted to COA office but were not within five working days from execution of the contract.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct all relevant City officials to ensure that all issues relevant to the implementation of 20 per cent Development Fund projects are resolved or addressed in the planning or development phase of the projects to avoid delays in their implementation and facilitate the full attainment of the desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes of the City.
observations.managementAction: The City Planning and Development ensured that all issues relevant to the implementation of 20 per cent Development Fund projects are resolved or addressed.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that the City Mayor direct the General Service Officer (GSO) and other concerned officials to make a study or analysis of the costs and benefits of disposing or repairing the defective vehicles. For vehicles determined or which could be established to be beyond repair, we recommended that their disposal be immediately initiated by the City in accordance with existing rules and regulations on disposal of unserviceable properties. For serviceable vehicles and those which could still be reasonably repaired, we again recommended that the City Mayor require the GSO to exert more effort to find the relevant Deeds of Sale, Certificate of Registration and other necessary documents and to immediately comply with other LTO’s requirements to facilitate the vehicles’ registration in the City’s name.
observations.managementAction: The Management committed to exert more efforts to comply with the recommendations. The City General Services Office tried to locate the relevant documents for vehicle’s registration but was not able to locate them due to restricted operations in different offices brought about by COVID-19.
observations.reason: The Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property was submitted to COA office on March 23, 2022. Disposal in accordance with existing rules and procedures were not yet undertaken. The City General Services Office was not able to locate the documents for registration due to restricted operations in different offices because of COVID-19.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor require the CSWDO to: a. submit evidence to contradict the allegations of beneficiaries that they did not receive the full amount, b. investigate further and provide proof to COA, and c. coordinate with the concerned Barangay Officials to facilitate submission to COA of updated contact numbers of beneficiaries for the Audit Team’s further confirmation.
observations.managementAction: The CSWDO was already coordinating with the identified barangays and advised the Barangay Chairmen to provide relevant documentary proof that the financial assistance that were received by them on behalf of the beneficiaries were actually distributed and hand-carried to the identified individuals. Xerox copy of the Official Receipt was submitted. Proof of receipt was submitted. Updated contact numbers of beneficiaries were submitted.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that the City Mayor issue an Executive Order creating the M&E Team as specified in Item 5.0 of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01 that will assess and report on the outcome and gender responsiveness of City’s implemented GAD policies, programs and projects to assist concerned City officials to make informed decisions.
observations.managementAction: The secretariat/members of GAD Focal Point System already attended a two-day Virtual Training on GAD Planning and Budgeting, use of HGDG Tools organized by the DILG on March 8 and 9, 2022 and assured the Audit Team that they will attend a more comprehensive training on the use of HGDG Tools to build more competence in the conduct of gender analysis of programs and projects for purposes of GAD budget attribution. The M&E Team was submitted.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant and the OIC,
General Services Office to analyze and reconcile the discrepancy of
P3,445,922,104.24 between the Accounting records and the RPCPPE, and make
the necessary adjustments.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant to make the
necessary adjusting entries to recognize the assets in the General Fund and Trust
Fund books, in accordance with GAM for LGUs, Volume I.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant to prepare a
Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to reclassify the balance of advance collection of
business taxes from the Other Payables account to Other Deferred Credits
account to reflect accurate and reliable account balances.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the:
a) Acting City Treasurer to require the business taxpayers to submit Sworn
Statement of all their gross sales/receipts not only from the City
Government for the preceding Calendar Year and other pertinent
records/information for the determination of gross receipts;
b) City Accountant to submit to the Acting City Treasurer a List of Payments
made to contractors/suppliers who transacted with the City Government in
the preceding calendar year to serve as one of the bases in the
assessment of business taxes in the current year;
c) OIC-Business Permit and Licensing Officer to compute any possible
interest and surcharges; and
d) Acting City Treasurer to re-assess the declared gross receipts of the
concerned suppliers in calendar year (CY) 2020 and validate the reported
under collection totaling P2,975,335.82. After which, immediately enforce
the collection of underpayments including penalties and surcharges, if
there are any, pursuant to the rates provided in the City’s Revised Revenue
Code, otherwise, Notice of Charge will be issued by the Audit Team.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC)
to explain/justify the material discrepancies between the prices of the basic
commodities procured by the City in response to the COVID-19 and their
suggested retail prices issued by the authorized government agencies during the
price freeze implementation, to prevent suspension and/or disallowance in audit.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant to properly
record the purchases of welfare goods under the Welfare Goods for Distribution
account, and the distributed portion to Welfare Goods Expenses, which will be
closed out to the Transfer from General Fund of Unspent DRRMF account at the
end of the year for proper monitoring of receipts, issuances, and balances of
goods procured out of the prior year’s unexpended balances of LDRRMF.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the OIC-City Budget Officer to ensure
that only expenditures directly related to disaster risk management are charged
against the LDRRMF, while administrative expenses are charged instead against the
General Fund appropriations of respective City Offices.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the BAC Secretariat to submit to the
Audit Team the justifications on its inability to comply with the posting requirement
under GPPB Circular No. 01-2020, for further evaluation.

We reiterated our previous year’s audit recommendation that the City Mayor direct
the Head, BAC Secretariat to comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-
001 on the submission of copies of contracts and their supporting documents to the
Auditor within five working days from the execution thereof, to facilitate the prompt
review and evaluation of the contracts, and the early communication to Management
on the deficiencies noted, if there are any.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct all concerned City officials to ensure
that all issues relevant to the implementation of 20 per cent Development Fund
projects are resolved or addressed in the planning or development phase of the
projects to avoid delays in their implementation, and facilitate the full attainment
of the desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes of the
City.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the General Service Officer (GSO) and
other concerned officials to make a study or analysis of the costs and benefits of
disposing or repairing the defective vehicles. For vehicles beyond repair or
repairs of which would not be practical, it was recommended that their disposal in
accordance with existing rules and procedures be initiated by the City.

For serviceable vehicles and those which could still be reasonably repaired, we
recommended that the City Mayor require the GSO to exert more effort to find the
relevant Deeds of Sale, Certificate of Registration and other necessary documents
or to immediately comply with other LTO’s requirements to facilitate or complete
the vehicles’ registration in the City’s name.

However, we recommended that the City Mayor require the CSWDO to:
a. submit evidence to account the refund made by one beneficiary;
b. investigate further and provide proof to substantially contradict the
allegations of two beneficiaries that they did not receive the full amount
of P6,500.00, and one beneficiary who claimed that he did not receive
any assistance from the DSWD; and
c. coordinate with the concerned Barangay officials to facilitate the
submission to the Commission on Audit (COA) of the updated contact
numbers of beneficiaries, for the Audit Team’s further or expanded
confirmation.

We recommended that the City Mayor issue an Executive Order or Administrative
Order creating the M&E Team as specified in Item 5.0 of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA
JMC No. 2013-01 that will assess and report on the outcome and gender
responsiveness of City’s implemented GAD policies, programs and projects to
assist concerned City officials to make informed decisions.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the 18 City Offices to submit to the General Services Office (GSO) the records of PPE under their custody to facilitate the complete and submission of the RPCPPE to the Auditor, pursuant to Section 124 of MNGAS, Volume I.
observations.managementAction: submitted PPE reports to GSO The RPCPPE was prepared and submitted to Commission on Audit (COA) Inventory on January 21, 2021.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the Head of GSO, in coordination with the City Social Worker and/or Development Office and/or City Disaster Risk and Reduction Management Office, to prepare and submit the RSMI and RIS to the City Accountant to support the credits made to Welfare Goods for Distribution amounting to P3,229,000.00 for the Audit Team’s validation. We also recommended that henceforth, the City Accountant, Heads of the General Services Office, City Social Worker and Development Office and or City Disaster Risk and Reduction Management Office comply with the accounting and reporting guidelines on the receipt and utilization of the LDRRMF as prescribed in COA Circular No. 2014-002 dated April 15, 2014, to present fairly the expense and inventory accounts of the City’s welfare goods for distribution.
observations.managementAction: Compliance with accounting and reporting guidelines observed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the focal persons responsible to expedite the implementation of the BUB-funded projects such as the improvement of TB-DOTS facility and procurement of school furniture.
observations.managementAction: The RSMI and RIS pertaining to P3,229,000.00 were already submitted to the City Accountant. The City Health Office is not aware of the fund and was only informed about it during the interview made by COA personnel. The proposed improvement will be implemented immediately. The amount of P1,200,000.00 was already refunded to DOH per Check No. 877074 dated March 19, 2021.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the Head, Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) Secretariat to comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-001 on the submission of copies of contracts and their supporting documents to the Auditor within five working days from the execution thereof, to facilitate the prompt review and evaluation of the contracts, and the early communication to Management on the deficiencies noted, if there are any.
observations.managementAction: Submitted the approved supporting documents on July 6, 2020, for 16 infrastructure projects implemented in CY 2019.
observations.reason: The required information were submitted for 16 infrastructure projects implemented in CY 2019. Reiterated in observation no. 9, Part II of the Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the: a. Local School Board (LSB) to limit the utilization of the SEF only to the programs, projects and activities that are in favor of a public elementary and secondary schools, pursuant to DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2017, as further interpreted under DILG Opinion No. 29, s. 2018; and b. City Budget Officer to restore the amounts inappropriately charged against the SEF from the General Fund amounting to P6,031,690.00, otherwise, this will be disallowed in audit.
observations.managementAction: The City Accountant and Budget Officer discussed the budget and expenses of CCT. There is sufficient General Fund budget and agreed that CCT SHS expenses can be charged to this allocation. The CCT expenses from January to March 2020 totaling P1,759,562.61 were already transferred per Check No. 1026134 dated December 22, 2020. Effective April 1, 2020, CCT SHS expenses will be funded directly from CCT General Fund budget. The amount of P6,031,690.00 CCT SHS expenses for CY 2019 charged against the SEF were already transferred from General Fund to SEF per Check No. 978762 dated March 5, 2020.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the OIC-CENRO, OIC-CENRO and City Accountant to submit to the COA Audit Team the documents that will facilitate complete review and technical evaluation of the contract for the garbage hauling services and post-audit of the payments made in relation thereto, providing, among others, the: a. brand name, model, make/country of origin, hp, piston displacement, and capacity and number of units of dump trucks to be and actually used by the Contractor; b. complete descriptions/specifications (age, condition, brand, etc.) and number of units of all other equipment to be and actually rented/used; c. measurement in driver’s trip tickets; d. estimated volume in cubic meters of garbage to be and actually hauled from area of operation, including the basis for such estimates; and e. distance covered by one complete route.
observations.managementAction: The OIC, CENRO submitted the list of trucks as per contract with corresponding specification. The required information were incorporated in the revised technical specification. The OIC, CENRO explained that number of motor vehicles owned by the City is 180, of which, 87 or 48 per cent were still green plated and registered in the name of previous owners. She further explained that the City is facilitating the transfer of ownership of the 77 units out of the 87 to comply with Item e.3, Section 361 of the GAAM, Volume I.
observations.reason: The required information were incorporated in the revised specification. The OIC, CENRO is facilitating transfer of ownership of the vehicles.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor require the General Services Officer to exert more effort to look for the relevant Deeds of Sale, Certificate of Registration and other documents that will facilitate the transfer of the registration of the 122 motor vehicles in the name of the City.
observations.managementAction: The OIC-GSO submitted an updated list of motor vehicles and commented that the number of motor vehicles owned by the City is 180, of which, 87 or 48 per cent were still green plated and registered in the name of previous owners. She further explained that the City is facilitating the transfer of ownership of the 77 units out of the 87 to comply with Item e.3, Section 361 of the GAAM, Volume I.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor ensure the active participation in the development and maintenance of a GAD Database containing statistics and sex disaggregated data being used in the city’s gender responsive planning, programming, and policy formulation, in compliance with Item 4.1.B.1 of Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 of PCW, DILG, DBM and NEDA. We also recommended that the City Mayor ensure that the appropriation for GAD is at least five per cent of the City’s total annual budget.
observations.managementAction: Management informed that at present, the City Planning and Development Management Office already designed a system for family profiling of the City’s population, and to establish an accurate profile of every individual and every household in Tagaytay City. The City, through the assistance of the DILG will implement the Community Based Monitoring System (CBMS) to update the baseline data on the profile of the City’s population. The City provided a copy of the required Executive Order to create the M&E Team. The City Mayor informed that the 5 per cent allocation for the 2020 GAD programs will be complied with accordingly.
observations.reason: The system for family profiling was not yet completed. The CBMS implementation and baseline data update are ongoing.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor require the 18 City Offices to submit to the
General Services Office the records of PPE under their custody to facilitate the
complete physical inventory taking of PPE by the City Inventory Committee and
the General Services Office (GSO) and the preparation and submission of the
RPCPPE to the Auditor pursuant to Section 124 of the MNGAS, Volume I.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the Head of General Services Office,
in coordination with the City Social Worker and Development Office and/or City
Disaster Risk and Reduction Management Office, to prepare and submit the RSMI
and RIS to the City Accountant to support the credits made to account Welfare
Goods for Distribution amounting to P3,229,000.00 for the Audit Team’s validation.

We also recommended that henceforth, the City Accountant, Heads of the General
Services Office, City Social Worker and Development Office and or City Disaster
Risk and Reduction Management Office comply with the accounting and reporting
guidelines on the receipt and utilization of the LDRRMF prescribed in COA
Circular No. 2014-002 dated April 15, 2014 to present fairly the expense and
inventory accounts of the City’s welfare goods for distribution.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the focal persons responsible to
expedite the implementation of the BUB-funded projects such as the improvement
of TB-DOTS facility and procurement of school furniture.

We also reiterated our previous recommendation that the City Mayor direct the
City Budget Officer, City Planning and Development Officer and other concerned
officials to study the legality and practicality of augmenting the BUB funds from
the City’s own budget considering that the construction of the clinics had already
been determined as priority projects in the City’s LPRAP. Otherwise, we
recommended that the City Mayor instruct the concerned officials to return
without further delay the amount of P1,200,000.00 to the DOH so that the amount
could be channeled to other priority projects of the government.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the Head, BAC Secretariat to comply
with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-001 on the submission of copies of
contracts and their supporting documents to the Auditor within five working days
from the execution thereof, to facilitate the prompt review and evaluation of the
contracts, and the early communication to management on the deficiencies noted,
if any.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the:
a. Local School Board (LSB) to limit the utilization of the SEF only to programs,
projects and activities that are in favor of a public elementary and secondary
schools pursuant to DEPED-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1 s. 2017, as further
interpreted under DILG Opinion No. 29, s. 2018 dated June 6, 2018;
b. City Budget Officer to restore the amounts inappropriately charged against
the SEF from the General Fund amounting to P6,031,690.00, otherwise, this
will be disallowed in audit.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the Bids and Awards Committee, OIC-
CENRO and City Accountant to submit to the COA Audit Team documents that
will facilitate complete review and technical evaluation of the contract for the
garbage hauling services and post-audit of the payments made in relation thereto,
providing, among others, the:
a. Brand name, model, make/country of origin, hp, piston displacement,
capacity and number of units of dump trucks to be and actually used by
the Contractor;
b. Complete descriptions/specifications (age, condition, brand, etc.) and
number of units of all other equipment to be and actually rented/used;
c. The measurement in kilometers of the total distance covered by one
complete route for all the required routes to be and actually travelled;
d. Estimated volume in cubic meters of garbage to be and actually hauled
from area of operation, including the basis for such estimates; and
e. Distance covered by one complete route.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the General Services Officer to exert
more effort to look for the relevant Deeds of Sale, Certificate of Registration and
other documents that will facilitate the transfer of the 122 motor vehicles in its
name.

We recommended that the City Mayor, as the Chairman of GAD Focal Point
System (GFPS), direct the Technical Working Group (TWG) or Secretariat, which is
composed of the different offices within the City, to actively participate in the
development and maintenance of a GAD Database containing statistics and sex
disaggregated data systematically gathered, regularly updated and subjected to
gender analysis for responsive City-wide GAD related planning, programming,
and policy formulation in compliance with Item 4.1.B.1 of Joint Memorandum
Circular No. 2013-01 of PCW, DILG, DBM and NEDA.

We also recommended that the City Mayor ensure that the appropriation for GAD
is at least 5 per cent of the City’s total annual budget.

1. We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant and the OIC, General Services
Office to analyze and reconcile the discrepancy between the Accounting records and RPCPPE, and
make the necessary adjustments.
2. We also recommended that the General Services Office maintain Property Cards for property, plant
and equipment in existing PPELCs in compliance with Section 114 of the MNGAS, Volume I and the
Accounting Office to complete and update the PPE Ledger Cards (PPELCs) to expedite the
reconciliation of PPE between the Accounting and Property records.
3. We further recommended that the OIC, General Services Office prepare the Inventory and
Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) and the Waste Material Report for
unserviceable properties.
observations.managementAction:
Started retrieval of old PPE records the OIC, General
Services Office are still in the process of
reconciliation. The target date of completion of
the reconciliation is March 31, 2020.

Started the preparation of Property Cards
and prepared the cards for updating.

The OIC, General Services Office prepared the
IIRUP and the Waste Material Report for
unserviceable properties. Those unserviceable
properties identified to be worth P3,709,345.75
were submitted already to the City Accountant
for recording in the books.
observations.reason:
The City Accountant and the OIC, General Services Office are still in the
process of reconciliation. The target date of completion of the
reconciliation is March 31, 2020.

Preparation of Property Cards is not yet complete as of December
31, 2019.

observations.recommendation:
4. We recommended that the City Accountant adhere to the Conceptual Framework of the PPSAS,
Volume I, such that transactions and other events are recognized in financial statements when they
occur and not only when cash or its equivalent is received or paid to present the true financial
position of the City at year-end.
5. We also recommended that the City Accountant compute and record the depreciation pertaining to
prior years' completed projects.
6. We also recommended that the account Construction in Progress-Infrastructure Assets be taken up
in the books to record infrastructure projects while still in the process of construction in compliance
with COA Circular No. 2015-09 dated December 1, 2015.
observations.managementAction:
Depreciation expenses pertaining to prior years' completed projects were already
computed and recorded in the books per Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) No. 100-2019-12-44596 dated
December 12, 2019.

Infrastructure projects still in the process of construction were now taken up in the books as
Construction in Progress-Infrastructure Assets.

observations.recommendation:
1. We recommended that the City Mayor or her authorized representative seek clarification from the
Donor Agency if paragraph 4.g of the Deed of Donation can still be applied, or else, we
recommended to return to the Donor Agency the unexpended balance of P4,922,215.45 so it could
be allocated for other equally important government projects.
observations.managementAction:
The unexpended balance was already returned to the Donor Agency per check no. 876632
dated September 24, 2019.

observations.recommendation:
8. We reiterated our previous recommendation that the City Mayor direct the City Budget Officer, City
Planning and Development Officer and other concerned officials to study the legality and
practicality of augmenting the BuB funds from the City’s own budget considering that the
construction of the clinics had already been determined as priority projects in the City’s Local
Poverty Reduction Action Plan (LPRAP). Otherwise, we recommended that the City Mayor instruct the
concerned officials to return without further delay the amount of P2,200,000.00 to the DOH so that the
amount could be channeled to other priority projects of the government.
observations.managementAction:
The One Million Pesos allocation for the purchase of armchairs had already been implemented in CY
2020. The armchairs had been purchased and delivered to Tagaytay City Science National High
School (TCSNHS) and Tagaytay City Central School, respectively.

As to the P100,000.00 intended for the improvement of TB-DOTS facility maintained by the City
Health Office (CHO), the City Health Office is not aware of the fund and was only informed about it
during the interview made by COA personnel. The proposed improvement will be implemented
immediately.

For the P1,200,000.00 intended for the construction of school clinic in Sambong Elementary School, a
letter of inquiry had been sent to the DOH Secretary, if whether the City can implement the project with
funding support from the City Government.
observations.reason:
The One Million Pesos allocation for the purchase of armchairs had already been implemented in
CY 2020. The armchairs had been purchased and delivered to Tagaytay City Science National High
School (TCSNHS) and Tagaytay City Central School, respectively.

As to the P100,000.00 intended for the improvement of TB-DOTS facility maintained by the City
Health Office (CHO), the City Health Office is not aware of the fund and was only informed about it
during the interview made by COA personnel. The proposed improvement will be implemented
immediately.

For the P1,200,000.00 intended for the construction of school clinic in Sambong Elementary School, a
letter of inquiry had been sent to the DOH Secretary, if whether the City can implement the project with
funding support from the City Government.

The school clinic was not constructed and the fund was not yet returned to the DOH.
Reiterated in Observation No. 3, Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation:

job order workers under MOOE in the preparation of the ensuing year’s annual budget and the City
Accountant to record payments made to appropriate MOOE accounts.

observations.recommendation: 4. property, plant and equipment in compliance with Section 114 of the MNGAS,

We recommended that the City Accountant adhere to the Conceptual Framework of
the PPSAS, Volume I, such that transactions and other events are recognized in

received or paid to present the true financial position of the of the City at year-end.
We also recommended that the City Accountant compute and record the
depreciation pertaining to prior years completed projects; and
We also recommended that the account Construction in Progress-Infrastructure
Assets be taken up in the books to record infrastructure projects while still in the

We recommended that the City Mayor or her authorized representative seek
clarification from the Donor Agency if paragraph 4.g of the Deed of Donation can
still be applied, or else, we recommended to return to Donor Agency the
unexpended balance of P4,922,215.45 so it could be allocated for other equally

We reiterated our previous recommendation that the City Mayor direct the City

officials to study the legality and practicality of augmenting the BuB funds from the
Citys own budget considering that the construction of the clinics had already been

classification of wages to job order workers under MOOE in the preparation of the

1. We strongly reiterated our prior recommendation that the Officer In Charge (OIC)-General Services Officer (GSO) and the City Inventory Committee prioritize the completion of the physical inventory of all PPE.
2. We further recommended that the City Accounting Office continue its efforts to determine the details of the PPE totaling CY 2018 as part of additional PPE accounts.
observations.managementAction: The Report on Physical Count of year’s Property, Plant and Equipment (RCPPE) was submitted to COA on February 28, 2019.
observations.reason:
Reiterated in Audit Observation No. 1, Page No. 33
The amount of other PPE accounts, the amount of P484,867,523.37 still did not have PPE Ledger Cards P102,209,807.30 were taken up for General Fund Reconciliation Report.

observations.recommendation: 3. We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Engineering Office and other concerned offices to immediately complete the project documents for the construction of the new Ospital ng Tagaytay and slaughterhouse to prevent further delay in the implementation of the projects and thereafter ensure that all projects are implemented on time in accordance with the City’s Annual Investment Plan.
observations.managementAction: The amount of P53,879,728.00 was appropriated to Development Projects per Supplemental Budget No. 1 CY 2018 as part of acquisition of lot with total amount of P126,250,000.00 likewise the said amount (P53,879,728.00) was already disbursed for CY 2018 as per submitted list of disbursements under Lot Acquisition.

observations.recommendation: 5. We recommended that the City Mayor direct the Tourism Officer and other concerned City Officials to expedite the implementation of the projects for the improvement and beautification of the Picnic Grove in order for it to attract more tourists, and thus maximize its income potential.
observations.managementAction: Five out of the 11 projects for the Improvement and beautification of the Picnic Grove were already implemented. Projects for the Improvement and beautification of the Picnic Grove for six projects have corresponding re-requests for funding from the Department of Tourism.

observations.recommendation: 6. We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant to strictly comply with BIR Revenue Memorandum Circular No. 23-2012 by ensuring that the amount of all taxes withheld are remitted in full on or before the 15th day of the following month.
observations.managementAction: The City Mayor instructed that the amount of the Due to BIR account as of the ending balance were all remitted during the year 2018. We likewise recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City Accountant to exert extra effort to analyze, trace and determine the details of Due to BIR account amounting to P5,067,361.14 and cause its immediate remittance to the BIR and/or make the necessary adjustments in the City’s books of accounts as the case maybe.

observations.recommendation: 8. We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City Accountant to prepare a Journal Entry Voucher to debit the Cash in Bank-Local Currency, Current Account and credit the Accounts Payable accounts in the amount of P2,404,105.00.
observations.managementAction: Ten (10) stale checks with total amount of P2,404,105.00 were already adjusted during CY 2018. Three checks amounting to P2,068,103.25 were replaced. Reminders to payees to return the stale checks were undertaken by the City Treasurer.

observations.recommendation: 11. We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Agriculture Officer to submit to the City Accountant the Project Status Report on the Cattle Dispersal Program of the City so that proper adjustments of the Breeding Stocks account could be made. If relevant documents could not anymore be found despite diligent efforts, we recommended that the City Mayor direct concerned officials to request from the COA authority to write-off the account, supported by available records, extent of validation made and certification and reasons why supporting documents could not be located, pursuant to COA Circular No. 97-001 dated February 5, 1997 as amended by COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.
observations.managementAction: The Breeding Stocks account amounting to P913,441.18 was already adjusted per Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) No. 100-2018-12-35174 dated December 31, 2018. The City Agriculture Officer submitted the Project Status Report on the Cattle Dispersal Program of the City to the City Accountant and adjustments were effected.

observations.recommendation: 12. We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Human Resource and Management Officer (HRMO) and other concerned officials to assess the nature of works and exposure to hazards of employees assigned at the Ospital ng Tagaytay, City Health Office and other health-related offices and make a proposal to include in the budget the payment of subsistence, hazard and laundry allowances.
observations.managementAction: The amount of employees engaged in health and other health-related works at the City Health Office and Ospital ng Tagaytay are now being paid of the subsistence allowance, hazard allowance and laundry allowance. The amount of P2,026,150.00 was included in the General Fund (GF) Annual Budget for CY 2019 for the subsistence, hazard and laundry allowances.

observations.recommendation: 13. We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant to record the allowances of BHWs, BNSs, DCW, OSCA Heads, PDAO and Persons With Disabilities (PWD) Affairs Office and the Municipal Judge to Other Professional Services account and ensure that only disbursements in the nature of aid to indigents or to sectors in need of assistance are charged against the Donations account. 14. We likewise recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City Budget Officer and other concerned officials to consider charging against the SEF the allowances of Day Care Workers and other expenses related to the ECCD Program.
observations.managementAction: Allowances of BHWs, BNSs, Day Care Workers, etc. were already recorded under the Other Professional Services Allowances of Day Care Workers and other expenses related to ECCD Program were already included in the Annual SEF Budget. The City Mayor instructed the City Budget Officer and other concerned officials to consider charging against the SEF the allowances of Day Care Workers and other expenses related to the ECCD Program.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor require the OIC-General Services Officer and the City Inventory Committee to prioritize the completion of the physical inventory of all PPE. We further recommended that the City Accounting Office continue its efforts to determine the details of the PPE totaling P587,365,828.67

observations.recommendation:
We strongly reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the Officer
In Charge (OIC)-General Services Officer (GSO) and the City Inventory
Committee prioritize the completion of the physical inventory of all PPE. We
further recommended that the City Accounting Office continue its efforts to
determine the details of the PPE totaling P587,365,828.67.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Engineering Office
and other concerned offices to immediately complete the project documents for
the construction of the new Ospital ng Tagaytay and slaughterhouse to prevent
further delay in the implementation of the projects and thereafter ensure that all
projects are implemented on time in accordance with the City’s AIP.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Budget Officer,
City Planning and Development Officer and other concerned officials to study
the legality and practicality of augmenting the BuB funds from the City’s own
budget considering that the construction of the clinics had already been
determined as priority projects in the City’s LPRAP. Otherwise, we
recommended that the City Mayor instruct the concerned officials to return the
amount of P2,200,000.00 to the DOH so that the amount could be channeled to other
priority projects of the government.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Tourism Officer
and other concerned City Officials to expedite the implementation of the projects
for the improvement and beautification of the Picnic Grove in order for it to
attract more tourists, and thus maximize its income potential.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant to
strictly comply with BIR Revenue Memorandum Circular No. 23-2012 by
ensuring that the amount of all taxes withheld are remitted in full on or before
the 15th day of the following month.
We likewise recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City
Accountant to exert extra effort to analyze, trace and determine the details of the
Due to BIR account amounting to P5,067,361.14 and cause its immediate
remittance to the BIR and/or make the necessary adjustments in the City’s
books of accounts as the case maybe.

We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City Accountant to
prepare a Journal Entry Voucher to debit the Cash in Bank-Local Currency,
Currency Account and credit the Accounts Payable accounts in the amount of
P2,404,105.00. We likewise recommended that the City Mayor direct the City
Treasurer to inform in writing the concerned payees to return the checks within the
set deadline with the advice that failure to do so will compel the City to take the
necessary steps to revert the payables to the proper funds.

We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City Agriculture
Officer to exert efforts to retrieve all documents pertaining to the distribution of
the cattle to beneficiaries in order for the City Accountant to adjust the dormant
Breeding Stocks account in the books of the City amounting to P913,441.18.
If relevant documents could not anymore be found despite diligent efforts,
we recommended that the City Mayor direct concerned the officials to request
from the COA authority to write-off the account, supported by available records,
extent of validation made and certification and reasons why supporting
documents could not be located, pursuant to COA Circular No. 97-001 dated
February 5, 1997 as amended by COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December
19, 2016.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Human Resource
and Management Officer (HRMO) and other concerned officials to assess the
nature of works and exposure to hazards of employees assigned at the Ospital ng
Tagaytay, City Health Office and other health-related offices and make proposal
to include in the budget the payment of subsistence, laundry and/or hazard
allowance to eligible City Health Workers.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant to
record the allowances of the BHW, BNS, DCW, OSCA Heads, PDAO Focal
Persons and authorized national government officials to Other Professional
Services account and ensure that only disbursements in the nature of aid to
indigents or to sectors in need of assistance are charged against the Donations
account.
We likewise recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City Budget
Officer and other concerned officials to consider charging against the SEF the
allowances of Day Care Workers and other expenses related to the ECCD
Program.
We recommended that the City Mayor require the City Accountant to exert extra efforts to ensure that all accounts are timely submitted to the Commission on Audit in compliance with the COA RRSA.
observations.managementAction: 2017 accounts had been timely submitted to the COA for CY 2016 AAR

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the members of the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC), OIC-GSO and other concerned officials to consolidate all requests from the City, City Health Office and Ospital ng Tagaytay for drugs and medicines and medical supplies with long shelf life/ commonly or regularly being used in the hospital and pursue competitive bidding in the procurement thereof, unless resort to shopping or other alternative mode of procurement is warranted by the conditions set forth by the IRR of RA No. 9184. Should resort to shopping or other alternative mode of procurement is necessary, we recommended that the City Mayor advise the OIC-GSO to expand the solicitation of proposals to include other reputable, well-known and eligible suppliers of drugs and medicines and medical supplies existing in the market to ensure that the City would obtain the lowest costs for the items.
observations.managementAction: Solicitation had been extended to other qualified suppliers.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor require the City Treasurer and other concerned officials to study and consider shifting to automated payroll system to ensure better control over handling of cash and administrative efficiency.
observations.managementAction: Mode of processing at LBP-Tagaytay of City employees' data and other requirements is on-going, in preparation to shifting to automated payroll system

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Accountant and the City Treasurer immediately submit to the Audit Team the lacking documents to facilitate complete auditorial and technical review and avoid suspension or disallowance of the transactions. We likewise recommended that the City Accountant and the City Treasurer be instructed to comply with all the necessary requirements of the Bureau of Internal Revenue and Register of Deeds for the titling of the subject properties in the name of the City of Tagaytay.
observations.managementAction: The required documents had already been submitted to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Budget Officer and other concerned officials to restore to the SEF the amount of P41,524,200.00 from the General Fund subject to applicable budget rules and procedures. Henceforth, we recommended that the City Mayor and the Local School Board ensure that only projects and programs falling within the ambit of RA 7160 are charged against the SEF.
observations.managementAction: An amount of P2,374,110.00 had already been transferred from GF to SEF. The remaining amount will be transferred in 2019.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor and other concerned officials reorganize the composition of the City LSB in accordance with Section 98 of the Local Government Code of 1991 to ensure that the mandated sectors or stakeholders are able to participate in the deliberation of the SEF budget and utilization.
observations.managementAction: The composition of the City LSB is now in accordance with Section 98 of the Local Government Code of 1991.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor advise them to ensure that thorough planning with the involvement of all stakeholders is conducted in the formulation of projects and prior to the submission of the funding requests to the National Government. We further recommended that the City Mayor request from concerned National Government Agencies for copies of Notice of Cash Allocation (NCA) or equivalent notice on the release of the BuB funds to the City and other relevant documents to support the recording thereof in the City’s books and for purpose of transparency and accountability.
observations.managementAction: Projects totaling P7,809,443.23 had been implemented on the CY 2016 AAR. Thereafter, we only meetings with concerned national government officials whose projects have been implemented during the year had been conducted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant and other concerned City officials to immediately coordinate with the donor agencies to determine the terms and conditions of the donations/subsidies. If it could be established that there is no contractual agreement as to the return of the funds to the trustor, it was recommended that the funds be reverted to the Unappropriated Surplus of the General Fund of the City pursuant to the COA-DBM Joint Circular No. 9-81. Otherwise, we recommended that the funds be returned to the Bureau of Treasury in compliance with Executive Order No. 431.
observations.managementAction: The funds have been reverted to the Unappropriated Surplus of the General Fund of the City pursuant to the COA-DBM Joint Circular No. 9-81.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor advise all concerned City Department Heads and other concerned officials to comply with AO No. 17 by purchasing common-use supplies and consumables directly from the PS-DBM and to resort to competitive bidding when the supplies are not available in the PS store as certified by the latter. We further recommended that reimbursements only resorted to when justified by circumstances and not prohibited by existing regulations.
observations.managementAction: Procurement were made through competitive bidding except when circumstances/ conditions warrant alternative mode of procurement. Reimbursements were only resorted to when justified by circumstances and not prohibited by existing regulations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the members of the City Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council thru the City Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer (CDRRMO) to conduct comprehensive planning and evaluation prior to finalizing the LDRRM Plan by taking into consideration all factors and relevant information needed for the effective implementation of the projects. We further recommended that the City Mayor direct the CDRRMO to ensure that the various projects in the LDRRM Plan are implemented accordingly to further improve the City's capacity to mitigate the adverse impacts of and/or respond to calamities.
observations.managementAction: Comprehensive planning had been conducted in the preparation of the LDRRM Plan. Various projects needed by the City to mitigate the impact of possible calamities had already been implemented by the City.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City General Service Officer and other concerned officials to comply with AO No. 17 by purchasing available quality common-use supplies from the PS-DBM during the year 2017 and to resort to competitive bidding or shopping only when the supplies are not available in the PS as certified by the latter.
observations.managementAction: The City purchased available quality common-used supplies from the PS-DBM during the year 2017.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor require the City Accountant to ensure that the Commission on Audit is provided with copies of contracts/Purchase Orders, together with the supporting documents, within five working days from execution/approval thereof, in compliance with COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.
observations.managementAction: Copies of contracts and/or Purchase Orders and supporting documents, including for procurement of Property, Plant and Equipment (PPE) were submitted on time to the Audit Team in compliance with COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.

observations.recommendation: We strongly reiterated our prior years’ recommendation that the City Mayor require the City Accountant and the OIC-General Services Officer to prioritize the reconciliation of the balances of their PPE records and the completion of the physical inventory of all PPE.
observations.reason: Management did not provide explanation on the non-implementation of the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor require the City Accountant to ensure that the Commission on Audit is provided with copies of contracts/Purchase Orders, together with the supporting documents, within five working days from execution/approval thereof, in compliance with COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.
observations.reason: Management did not provide explanation on the non-implementation of the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant and the concerned City officials to further evaluate the dormant accounts and consider the applicable remedies provided for under Executive Order No. 431, Joint Circular No. 9-81 and Section 122 of the GAAM, Volume I.
observations.reason: Management did not provide explanation on the non-implementation of the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor ensure that the City Accountant submit timely all accounts to the Commission on Audit as required under Section 7.2 of the Commission on Audit RRSA dated September 15, 2009.
observations.reason: Management did not provide explanation on the non-implementation of the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the members of the BAC Secretariat, City General Service Officer and other concerned officials to adopt a procurement strategy through which similar supplies are purchased in bulk and at reasonable frequency or on a quarterly basis, if practicable, in accordance with the City’s Annual Procurement Program, to ensure administrative efficiency and/or possible price discounts.
observations.reason: Management did not provide explanation on the non-implementation of the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor and the City Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer to ensure that the various projects and activities programmed in the LDRRM Plan are implemented accordingly, to further improve the City’s capacity to mitigate the adverse impacts of and/or respond to possible calamities.
observations.reason: Management did not provide explanation on the non-implementation of the audit recommendation.

Committee to prioritize the completion of the physical inventory of all PPE. We

exert extra efforts to ensure that all accounts are timely submitted to the COA in

OIC-GSO and other concerned officials to consolidate all requests from the City
Health Office and Ospital ng Tagaytay for drugs and medicines and medical
supplies with long shelf life/expiration which are commonly or regularly being

unless resort to shopping or other alternative mode of procurement is warranted

Should resort to shopping or other alternative mode of procurement is
necessary, we recommended that the City Mayor advise the OIC-GSO to expand
the solicitation of proposals to include other reputable, well-known and eligible
suppliers of drugs and medicines and medical supplies existing in the market to
ensure that the City would obtain the lowest costs for the items.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the City Treasurer and other
concerned officials to study and consider shifting to automated payroll system to
ensure better control over handling of cash and administrative efficiency.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant and the City
Treasurer to immediately submit to the Audit Team the lacking documents to
facilitate complete auditorial and technical review and avoid suspension or
disallowance of the transactions. We likewise recommended that they be
instructed to comply with all the necessary requirements of the BIR and Register
of Deeds for the titling of the subject properties in the name of the City of
Tagaytay.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Budget Officer and other
concerned officials to restore to the SEF the amount of £41,524,200.00 from the
General Fund subject to Chapter 3-Budgeting, Title V of RA No. 7160 and other
applicable budget rules and procedures. Otherwise, we recommended that
responsible officials be held personally liable for the refund of the amount.

We also recommended that henceforth, the City Mayor and the Local School
Board ensure that only projects and programs falling within the ambit of
Republic Act No. 7160 are charged against the SEF.

We recommended that the City Mayor and other concerned officials
reorganize the composition of the City LSB in accordance with Section 98 of the
Local Government Code of 1991 to ensure that the priorities of all mandated
sectors or stakeholders are taken into account in the deliberation of the SEF
budget and utilization.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Planning and Development
Office to coordinate with the concerned officials of the City Engineering Office,
DepEd, City Health Center and other City officials for the immediate resolution
of all issues on the implementation of the BUB projects. Thereafter, we
recommended that the City Mayor advise them to ensure that thorough planning
with the involvement of all stakeholders is conducted in the formulation of
projects and prior to the submission of the funding requests to the National
Government.

We further recommended that the City request from the concerned NGAs for
copies of Notice of Cash Allocation (NCA) or equivalent notice on the release of
the BuB funds to the City and other relevant documents to support the recording
thereof in the City’s books and for purposes of fransparency and accountability.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant and
other concerned City officials to immediately coordinate with the donor agencies
to determine the terms and conditions of the donations/subsidies. If it could be
established that there is no contractual agreement as to the return of the funds to
the trustor, it was recommended that the funds be reverted to the
Unappropriated Surplus of the General Fund of the City, pursuant to the COA-
DBM Joint Circular No. 9-81. Otherwise, we recommended that the funds be
returned to the Bureau of the Treasury, in compliance with Executive Order No.
431.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the members of the BAC
Secretariat, City General Service Officer and other concerned officials to adopt
a procurement strategy through which similar supplies are consolidated and
purchased in bulk on a quarterly or semestral basis, using competitive bidding
as mode of procurement unless resort to shopping or other alternative mode of
procurement is warranted by the conditions set forth by the IRR of RA No.
9184.

We further recommended that the City Mayor advise all the concerned City
officials to refrain from making reimbursements and ensure that except for
traveling expenses, all disbursements are in checks payable to "the
supplier/creditor.

We recommended that the City Mayor advise the members of the City Disaster
Risk Reduction Management Council, thru the City Disaster Risk Reduction and
Management Officer (CDRRMO), to conduct comprehensive planning and
evaluation prior to finalizing the LDRRM Plan by taking into consideration all
factors and relevant information needed for the effective implementation of the
projects.

We further recommended that the City Mayor direct the CDRRMO to ensure
that the various projects in the LDRRM Plan are implemented accordingly to
further improve the City’s capacity to mitigate the adverse impacts of and/or
respond to calamities.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Treasurer to expedite the updating of their records of real properties with multiple registrants/claimants and those exempted from taxes, and thereafter, furnish the City Accounting Office with complete certified list of the names of the taxpayers and the amounts due and collectible from them to enable the latter Office to have a reliable basis in recording the RPT and SET Receivables.
observations.managementAction: The book balances of the Real Property Tax (RPT) and SET schedules of the City Treasurer’s Office as of year-end.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the concerned officials to: a. prepare and submit to the Audit Team a letter requesting for COA’s approval for the writing-off of subject receivables, together with any available records and/or justification, why supporting documents could not be produced by the City; and b. ensure that the requirements for the write-off of accounts under COA Circular No. 97-001 dated February 5, 1997 are complied with, and the documents thereof form part of the request for write-off with the Commission on Audit.
observations.managementAction: The request for write-off submitted by the City is being evaluated by the Audit Team Leader.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant to refrain from directly recording the procurement or receipt of supplies and materials to expense account, but instead adopt the Perpetual Inventory System of accounting using as basis the SSMI being submitted by the General Services Office.
observations.managementAction: The City had adopted the Perpetual Inventory System of accounting starting year 2016.
observations.reason: Perpetual In- ventory System starting  year 2016.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant to revert the amount of R18,110,395.05 to the unappropriated surplus of the General Fund, and thereafter stop the practice of recognizing payables without complete documentation, in compliance with Section 4(s) of the MNGAS for LGUs and Section 4 of PD No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: The City Accountant completed the supporting documents of the subject Accounts Payable, and thus reversion was not anymore necessary. For Calendar Year 2016, the City Accountant ensured that all Accounts Payables were supported by substantial supporting documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Treasurer to record all deposits/collections and disbursements daily as required under Section 27 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume II, to ensure that the balance of cash is correctly reflected in the Cashbooks of the Government as of a given date.
observations.managementAction: All deposits/collections and disbursements of the City Treasurer’s Office were recorded daily.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant to institute means to effectively control and monitor the grant and liquidation —of  cash advances such as the installation of bulletin boards in the Accounting Office, showing relevant information on the cash advances made and their liquidation due dates and assigning to one  employee  the additional  duty  of reminding the concerned officials or demanding from them the liquidation of their cash advances.
observations.managementAction: Due to installation of several internal control over cash advances facilitated by the City Accounting Office, all cash advances during the year 2016 were timely liquidated.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the: a. City Accountant to take up the reconciling items identified in the Bank Reconciliation Statements (BRS) as of December 31, 2015, and thereafter see to it that the BRS are prepared near the close of each month to ensure that necessary adjustments are timely effected in the Cash in Bank-LCCA and Cash in Bank-LCTD accounts; and b. City Treasurer to update the Cashbooks by recording the reconciling items with a net amount of P1,441,133.11.
observations.managementAction: Both the City Accountant and the City Treasurer had taken up in their respective records all the reconciling items identified in audit as of year-end.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Treasurer to immediately seek the approval of the BLGF to continue maintaining deposits with the PNB-Tagaytay Branch, otherwise, coordinate with the concerned City officials to facilitate the transfer of the funds from the PNB to the Land Bank of the Philippines (LBP) or Development Bank of the Philippines (DBP) on or before June 1, 2016 in view of the latest issuance of the DOF on the matter.
observations.managementAction: Except for the balance of P990.00 in the PNB as of 2015 year-end, all funds in the PNB, specifically the Internal Revenue Allotment (IRA) of the City, were regularly being transferred from the PNB to the LBP. Nonetheless, as the IRA of the City is being deposited by the National Government to the PNB, the City Treasurer had already written the BLGF for authority.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City General Service Officer and other concerned Department Heads to comply with AO No. 17 by purchasing common-use supplies and consumables directly from the PS-DBM and to resort to competitive bidding or shopping only when the supplies are not available in the PS store as certified by the latter.
observations.managementAction: The City has started procuring supplies from the PS-DBM in November, 2016. All other procurements were from private vendors.
observations.reason: The City was still sorting out some issues with buying supplies from the DBM such as delivery, quality and timing of availability of supplies.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Engineer and other concerned officials to observe and comply with the publicity requirements for government programs, projects and activities as required under COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013 to promote good governance through transparency and accountability and to encourage public participation in their implementation.
observations.managementAction: The publicity required under COA Circular No. 2013-004 had been complied with by the City during the year.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the: a. City Treasurer, through the City Accountant, to submit to the COA the aforecited 44 cancelled checks for validation, and hence-forth, ensure that all cancelled checks are attached to the RCI and reported chronologically with the other checks issued with the word “Cancelled” clearly indicated therein; and, b. City Accountant to facilitate the submission to the COA of the RCI prepared in accordance with the requirements of the MNGAS.
observations.managementAction: The City Treasurer had submitted to the COA the subject 44 cancelled checks. She also ensured that all cancelled checks were attached to the RCI. Likewise, the City Accountant had been submitting to the COA the RCI in accordance with the MNGAS.
